title: Fix fab_review SDK misuse: split fat-object inputShape/outputShape from internal discriminatedUnion contracts
description: MCP SDK 1.29.0's validateToolOutput path passes undefined to safeParseAsync when registerTool inputSchema/outputSchema is not a z.object()-shaped raw shape, crashing on schema._zod access. fab_review currently passes z.discriminatedUnion(...) directly, which (a) causes runtime crashes for every action and (b) publishes JSON Schema with empty properties:{} so ToolSearch sees no fields. Fix by introducing flat ZodRawShape (FabReviewInputShape / FabReviewOutputShape) for SDK registration, while keeping the discriminatedUnion (FabReviewInputSchema / FabReviewOutputSchema) as the authoritative internal contract used inside the handler for runtime narrowing.

criteria: FabReviewInputShape and FabReviewOutputShape are exported from api-contracts.ts as flat ZodRawShape objects with `action: z.enum([...])` (required) and all union-branch fields marked `.optional()` with descriptive `.describe()` strings (including implicit-constraint hints such as 'required when action=approve')
FabReviewInputSchema and FabReviewOutputSchema (z.discriminatedUnion) remain exported and unchanged in semantics — they are now used only as the internal authoritative contract

review.ts registerTool call passes inputSchema=FabReviewInputShape and outputSchema=FabReviewOutputShape (the flat raw shapes), and handler entry calls `const narrowed = FabReviewInputSchema.parse(input)` for runtime strict narrowing before any branching logic
The misleading 'registerTool accepts AnySchema' comment around L24-26 of review.ts is removed

tools/list (and ToolSearch) returned input schema for fab_review contains a non-empty `properties` object including `action` plus every union-branch field; verifiable by an integration assertion on the published JSON Schema

Calling fab_review with action=approve but missing id produces a runtime ZodError whose message is semantically equivalent to the prior discriminatedUnion error (path includes 'id', issue type 'invalid_type' or 'required')
All 6 actions (list, approve, reject, modify, search, defer) exercise their happy path end-to-end and structuredContent passes FabReviewOutputShape validation
All pre-existing tests in packages/server/src/tools/review.test.ts and packages/server/__tests__/integration/fab-review.test.ts pass without modification of unrelated assertions
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fabric/server test -- review && pnpm --filter @fabric/server test -- fab-review
convergence.definition_of_done: fab_review tool is fully usable from any MCP client: ToolSearch sees a complete schema, every action runs without crashing, and invalid inputs surface clear validation errors.

files.change: Add new exports FabReviewInputShape and FabReviewOutputShape as flat ZodRawShape objects (z.object-friendly). Each shape: `action: z.enum([...all action literals...]).describe(...)` required; every other field across all union branches `.optional().describe(...)` with descriptions that document implicit constraints (e.g. 'Pending entry id; required when action=approve|reject|modify|defer'). Keep FabReviewInputSchema / FabReviewOutputSchema (discriminatedUnion) unchanged for internal use.

Replace `inputSchema: FabReviewInputSchema` with `inputSchema: FabReviewInputShape` and `outputSchema: FabReviewOutputSchema` with `outputSchema: FabReviewOutputShape`. Inside handler, immediately call `const narrowed = FabReviewInputSchema.parse(input)` and use `narrowed` everywhere downstream so runtime strictness matches the prior discriminatedUnion behaviour. Remove the misleading L24-26 comment about registerTool accepting AnySchema.

files.target: test cases asserting schema + handler narrowing
files.change: Add or extend tests asserting (a) registered inputSchema produces non-empty JSON Schema properties including `action` and union-branch fields, (b) action=approve with missing id throws ZodError naming `id`, (c) each action's happy path returns structuredContent that re-validates against FabReviewOutputShape.

Add integration assertion: after server registration, the published tool descriptor (as seen by an MCP client) includes a non-empty inputSchema.properties for fab_review (regression guard for the original SDK-misuse symptom). Ensure existing per-action assertions continue to pass.

implementation: 1. Read packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts and identify the FabReviewInputSchema / FabReviewOutputSchema discriminatedUnion definitions and the full enum of action literals.

2. Author FabReviewInputShape: `{ action: z.enum([...all action literals...]).describe('Action selector'), id: z.string().optional().describe('Pending id; required when action=approve|reject|modify|defer'), <all other union-branch fields>.optional().describe(...) }`. Mirror this for FabReviewOutputShape based on the union's output variants. Export both.

3. Update packages/server/src/tools/review.ts: change registerTool inputSchema/outputSchema to the new flat shapes; insert `const narrowed = FabReviewInputSchema.parse(input)` at handler top and refactor downstream usage to read from `narrowed` (preserving error precision and per-action branching).
4. Delete the L24-26 'registerTool accepts AnySchema' comment.

5. Add unit tests in review.test.ts covering: schema-shape assertion (properties non-empty, contains `action` + union branch fields), invalid-input narrowing (approve without id → ZodError mentioning `id`), and per-action structuredContent round-trip.
6. Extend packages/server/__tests__/integration/fab-review.test.ts with an integration assertion on the published JSON Schema (regression guard).
7. Run `pnpm --filter @fabric/server test` to verify all server tests (including pre-existing ones) pass.

manual_checks: Spawn server and call tools/list from an MCP client; confirm fab_review.inputSchema.properties contains action + union fields
Manually invoke each action against a fixture pending directory; confirm no '_zod' undefined-access stack trace appears
success_metrics: Zero runtime crashes across all 6 actions (was: 100% crash rate)
fab_review JSON Schema properties count >= 1 + count(union-branch field union) (was: 0)

regression: All pre-existing assertions in packages/server/src/tools/review.test.ts pass unchanged
All pre-existing assertions in packages/server/__tests__/integration/fab-review.test.ts pass unchanged
Other discriminatedUnion schemas in api-contracts.ts (events, ledger entries) are untouched and their consumers unaffected

reference.pattern: MCP SDK registerTool requires ZodRawShape (z.object-friendly) for input/output schema; internal narrowing uses the richer discriminatedUnion as authoritative contract
reference.examples: See other registerTool call sites in packages/server/src/tools/ (e.g. fab_extract_knowledge, fab_get_knowledge_sections) for the existing flat-shape pattern; mirror that convention while keeping the discriminatedUnion as private internal contract.

rationale.chosen_approach: Two-layer schema: flat ZodRawShape for SDK surface (satisfies validateToolOutput requirement) + discriminatedUnion for internal handler narrowing (preserves authoritative cross-field constraints). This keeps both the published JSON Schema rich (action + all branch fields visible to ToolSearch) and runtime validation strict.
alternatives_considered: Replace discriminatedUnion entirely with a flat z.object() and re-implement cross-field validation by hand — rejected: loses single source of truth and increases maintenance burden
Wrap the discriminatedUnion in z.object({ payload: discriminatedUnion }) — rejected: changes the public tool contract and breaks all existing callers
Patch the SDK or pin to an older version — rejected: out-of-band, fragile, and other tools work fine
decision_factors: Preserve existing tool-call shape (no client-visible API break)
Keep runtime validation as strict as before
Minimal blast radius — only fab_review and its tests touched
rationale.tradeoffs: Two parallel schema definitions for the same tool must stay in sync. Mitigated by collocating them in api-contracts.ts and adding a unit test that asserts every FabReviewInputShape field corresponds to a branch member of FabReviewInputSchema.

risks.description: FabReviewInputShape and FabReviewInputSchema drift over time as new actions are added
risks.mitigation: Add a unit test that programmatically diffs the keys of FabReviewInputShape against the union of branch keys of FabReviewInputSchema; fail loudly on drift
risks.fallback: Add an explicit comment block at the top of both definitions cross-referencing each other

risks.description: Pre-existing tests may rely on the inputSchema being an instance of ZodDiscriminatedUnion
risks.mitigation: Grep tests for `instanceof ZodDiscriminatedUnion` or `_def.typeName` checks before refactor; update assertions to read the published JSON Schema instead
risks.fallback: Keep both schemas exported (already planned) so any test importing FabReviewInputSchema directly still works

interfaces.name: FabReviewInputShape
interfaces.definition: { action: z.ZodEnum<[...]>; id?: z.ZodOptional<z.ZodString>; /* ...other union-branch fields all .optional() */ }
interfaces.purpose: Flat ZodRawShape passed to MCP SDK registerTool; produces a complete JSON Schema with non-empty properties

interfaces.name: FabReviewOutputShape
interfaces.definition: { /* union of output variant fields, all .optional() */ }
interfaces.purpose: Flat ZodRawShape for tool output schema registration

key_functions.purpose: Entry point: narrows input via FabReviewInputSchema.parse, then dispatches per action

commit.message_template: fix(server): split fab_review SDK shape from internal discriminatedUnion contract
